definition: Birth, conception, or arising, especially as conditioned by becoming in dependent arising.
notes: The project now keeps birth as the default because it is the most formula-ready rendering and leaves the term open to both practical here-and-now readings and broader renewed-existence readings without forcing one interpretation into every occurrence. Rebirth remains an acceptable alternate when the passage is clearly operating in a renewed-existence or comparative doctrinal register, and conception remains useful when the initiating side of the event should be heard more concretely. The Buddhadasa usage profile is important here: in dependent-arising analysis, jāti should stay available for the moment-to-moment birth of I and mine rather than being narrowed automatically to one later doctrinal frame. OSF editorial authority matters here as well, because it explicitly treats Buddhadasa as primary guidance for practical readings of jāti in dependent arising. Translators should therefore keep birth as the chain default, use rebirth only when the local frame clearly warrants it, and keep the movement from bhava into jāti into jarāmaraṇa stable across related formulas.

context_rules.context: In practical or Buddhadāsa-line explanation of the arising of I, mine, or renewed selfing
context_rules.notes: Keep the term open enough for immediate dependent-arising analysis.

context_rules.context: When the passage emphasizes the initiating or formative moment
context_rules.notes: Use when the arising side of the term should be foregrounded.

context_rules.context: When the passage is clearly using a renewed-existence or comparative doctrinal register
context_rules.notes: Use when the local frame explicitly requires that narrower reading.

context_rules.context: When the passage emphasizes the movement from becoming into aging-and-death
context_rules.notes: Keep the place of the link within the chain explicit.

version_notes: Strengthened to keep dependent-arising translation stable while still allowing narrower explanatory alternates where context clearly warrants them.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping jāti open to practical and immediate dependent-arising readings rather than forcing every occurrence into a purely biological or later doctrinal frame.
Supports treating Buddhadāsa as primary guidance for practical readings of jāti in dependent arising.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'birth' as the default rendering in dependent-arising contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not force 'rebirth' into every chain occurrence; use it only when the local doctrinal frame clearly requires that narrower reading.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the term from drifting into an automatic 'rebirth' reading that blocks practical here-and-now dependent-arising translation.
